name: Twin Sisters Vegetables Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup cooked kidney beans or 1 pint
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute the onion, garlic, oregano, and pepper in a small amount of water until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes. Set aside.
2. Dice the remaining vegetables (except the tomatoes) and add them to a large pot with the sauteed onion mixture. Add enough water to cover the vegetables. Include the bay leaves and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer until the vegetables are just tender, 10 to 15 minutes.
3. Puree the tomatoes in a blender or food processor and return them to a saucepan. Gently boil for 20 minutes to remove any acidic taste. Add the pureed tomatoes and cooked beans to the soup. Cook for an additional 30 minutes over low heat.
4. Refrigerate overnight to allow flavors to meld. Reheat and serve the next day.
83 calories, 0.5 grams fat per serving.
Variation: One-half cup each of corn and green beans can be added to the soup.
